explore.name: Kodanad Viewpoint
explore.description: A 220° panorama over the Moyar valley and the plains below — best at sunrise, when the mist lifts off the eastern slopes.
explore.details: The highest point in Kotagiri, Kodanad offers breathtaking views of the Nilgiri plateau and the plains stretching to the horizon. Ideal for sunrise photography.

explore.name: Catherine Falls
explore.description: A 250-ft double cascade, the second highest in the Nilgiris. Fullest after the monsoon — July to December.
explore.details: Named after Catherine Mary, a British settler's daughter, this waterfall is a spectacular double cascade visible from the Dolphin's Nose viewpoint. The trek down is moderate.

explore.name: Longwood Shola
explore.description: 116 hectares of rare surviving shola rainforest with a quiet birding trail — home to over 100 bird species.
explore.details: A pristine shola forest ecosystem with endemic flora and fauna. Home to the Nilgiri Pipit, Nilgiri Wood Pigeon, and over 60 bird species. Designated as a QCC (Quality Circle Council) conservation site.

explore.name: John Sullivan Memorial
explore.description: The restored 1819 bungalow where the Nilgiris story began — now a museum of colonial and tribal hill history.
explore.details: John Sullivan discovered Kotagiri in 1819 and established the first European settlement. His bungalow has been restored and opened as a museum showcasing the early colonial period and Kota tribal artifacts.

explore.name: Rangaswamy Peak & Pillar
explore.description: A sacred conical peak of the Irula tribe beside a striking 400-ft rock pillar — visible from Kodanad.
explore.details: Revered by the indigenous Irula tribe, Rangaswamy Peak stands as a spiritual landmark. The 400-ft pillar beside it is a geological marvel. Accessible by jeep trek.

explore.name: Nehru Park
explore.description: A scenic hilltop park with botanical garden, grassy meadows, and panoramic town views.
explore.details: Named after India's first PM, Nehru Park is Kotagiri's main public recreational space. Features a small botanical garden, toy train ride (weekends), and a pleasant picnic area.

explore.name: Nilgiri Mountain Railway
explore.description: Ride the UNESCO-listed toy train through Coonoor's hills — pair it with Elk Falls and a tea-estate walk on the way back.
explore.details: The UNESCO World Heritage Nilgiri Mountain Railway is a 46-km narrow-gauge train from Mettupalayam to Ooty, passing through Coonoor. A scenic 1.5-hour journey through tunnels, bridges, and tea estates.

activities.name: Catherine Falls Trek
activities.description: Half-day guided trek through tea gardens to the falls viewpoint.

activities.name: Tea Factory Tour
activities.description: Leaf to cup in 2 hours — watch withering, rolling and firing, then taste estate-fresh tea.

activities.name: Shola Birding Walk
activities.description: Early-morning walk in Longwood Shola with a naturalist. Binoculars provided.

activities.name: Sullivan Trail Historical Walk
activities.description: Walk stretches of the historic 1819 route into the hills — retraced every June.

activities.name: Jeep Safari to Rangaswamy Peak
activities.description: Off-road drive to Rangaswamy Peak with tribal elder storytelling and panoramic views.

activities.name: Tea/Spice Plantation Walk
activities.description: Morning walk through working tea and spice plantations with plucking demo and farm lunch.

transport.route: Via Mettupalayam or direct hill road

seasons.weather: Crisp air, clear valley views, low crowds (Nov–Feb especially)
seasons.tips: Carry warm layers for mornings and evenings. Rain gear optional.

seasons.weather: Pleasant escape from plains heat. June 15: Sullivan Trail expedition week.
seasons.tips: Summer heat from plains drives visitors up. Ideal for treks and activities.

seasons.weather: Misty & green. Lighter rain than Ooty thanks to rain shadow — waterfalls at best.
seasons.tips: Bring waterproof gear. Landslide risk on some roads. Better transport by afternoon.

seasons.weather: Never harsh. The classic subtropical highland climate of the Nilgiris.
seasons.tips: Kotagiri is quieter in off-season (Apr, Sep). Book early for peak months.
